default_annotator.report_text:
FIN7 leverages a number of targeted phishing techniques to initially exploit victims in the retail sector.
The actor primarily utilize malicious shortcut files (LNK) or visual basic scripts (VBS or VBE) to achieve code execution from within their lure The actor relies on users to open malicious LNK or Visual Basic script (VBS/VBE) files embedded within documents, triggering the infection.
The use of Visual Basic scripts (VBS or VBE) to execute malicious code within the victim system.
FIN7 appears to have pivoted from using OLE embedded LNK files to using OLE embedded CMD files. When executed, the CMD file writes JScript to "tt.txt" FIN7 uses CMD files embedded within documents, which require user interaction to execute malicious code.
The batch script then copies itself to "pp.txt", also under the current user's home directory, before running WScript using the JScript engine on the file The CMD file writes and then executes JScript code using the WScript engine to evaluate further instructions from the file.
The attacker now obfuscates that name and continues to break up the base64 string into multiple strings within an array The attacker continues to obfuscate the codebase by breaking up the base64-encoded string into multiple parts and renaming variables to evade detection.
